notes: Classical EGFR/ERBB ligands separated from neuregulins; ERBB2 is a ligand-orphan co-receptor.
Neuregulin/ERBB-axis ligands are not simple EGFR ligands but can still exercise the ERBB/EGFR PROGENy readout.
IL10-specific immune cytokine axis split out from the broader IL10/IL20/IL22 family.
IL19/IL20/IL24 epithelial-stromal axis; no longer pooled with IL10 immune signaling.
IL22 responsiveness is epithelial-restricted by IL22RA1; IL22RA2 is retained only as a decoy annotation.
IL26 is split from the IL10 family but remains context-review rather than a clean default row.
Common gamma chain cytokines; production v2 removes broad fibroblast sender assignment.
gp130/IL6ST cytokines; CRLF1 included for CLCF1 biology.
IL31 is kept separate from generic IL6ST/gp130 biology because it signals through IL31RA/OSMR.
Type I interferon receptor axis; JAK-STAT is generic and ISG/MHC-I would be a future cleaner readout.
Type II interferon receptor axis; JAK-STAT is generic and ISG/MHC-I would be a future cleaner readout.
Type III interferon receptor axis; JAK-STAT is generic and ISG/MHC-I would be a future cleaner readout.
Canonical TGFB/SMAD-like axis; TGFBR3 is annotated as accessory co-receptor rather than core kinase receptor.
TNF/LTA homotrimer biology through TNFR1/TNFR2 split from lymphotoxin-beta/LTBR biology.
Lymphotoxin-beta/LIGHT axis separated from TNFR1/TNFR2 rows.
Canonical TRAIL receptor pair, but PROGENy Trail transcriptomic readout remains context-review.
Canonical FAS/FASLG receptor pair, but PROGENy Trail transcriptomic readout remains context-review.
VEGFA axis with endothelial receivers; NRP1/2 are co-receptors, not sufficient signaling receptors.
VEGFR1-skewed ligands split from pan-VEGF rows.
VEGFR3/processed-VEGFC-D axis split from pan-VEGF rows.
Canonical-favoring WNT ligands retained for beta-catenin/PROGENy WNT readout.
Noncanonical WNT ligands are context-dependent and not clean beta-catenin/PROGENy WNT rows.
Context-dependent WNT ligands retained only in the audit manifest pending receptor-context refinement.
